lifecycle.c(3) Library Functions Manual lifecycle.c(3)

NAME

lifecycle.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions

bool lifecycle_init_once (lifecycle_t *lc)

bool lifecycle_shutdown (lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_shutdown_forever (lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_is_initialized (const lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_is_dead (const lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_reset (lifecycle_t *lc)
bool lifecycle_destroy_once (lifecycle_t *lc)
void lifecycle_destroy_commit (lifecycle_t *lc)

Function Documentation

void lifecycle_destroy_commit (lifecycle_t * lc)

Definition at line 215 of file lifecycle.c.

215                                                {
216   if (lc == NULL) {
217     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_commit: NULL lifecycle pointer");
218     return;
219   }
220   log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_commit: transitioning DESTROYING → UNINITIALIZED");
221   atomic_store_u64(&lc->state, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED);
222 }




References atomic_store_u64(), and log_dev.


Referenced by audio_stop_thread().






bool lifecycle_destroy_once (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 172 of file lifecycle.c.


172                                              {
173   if (lc == NULL) {
174     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: NULL lifecycle pointer");
175     return false;
176   }
177 
178   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
179   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING)) {
180     // If not initialized, nothing to destroy
181     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED) {
182       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: already uninitialized, nothing to destroy");
183       return false;
184     }
185 
186     // If dead, never allow destruction (already permanently shut down)
187     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
188       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: module is dead, no destruction allowed");
189       return false;
190     }
191 
192     // If already destroying, don't duplicate work (return false, let first destroyer finish)
193     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING) {
194       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: already destroying, skipping (first destroyer has priority)");
195       return false;
196     }
197 
198     // If initializing, skip destruction (init may still be in progress)
199     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
200       log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: still initializing, skipping destruction");
201       return false;
202     }
203 
204     // Unexpected state
205     log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: unexpected state: %d", expected);
206     return false;
207   }
208 
209   // Winner: state is now LIFECYCLE_DESTROYING
210   // Caller must call lifecycle_destroy_commit()
211   log_dev("[lifecycle] destroy_once: won CAS, transitioned to DESTROYING");
212   return true;
213 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), and log_dev.


Referenced by audio_stop_thread().






bool lifecycle_init (lifecycle_t * lc, const char *
  name)


Definition at line 6 of file lifecycle.c.


6                                                        {
7   if (lc == NULL) {
8     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: NULL lifecycle pointer");
9     return false;
10   }
11   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED;
12   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED)) {
13     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s already initialized (current state: %llu)", name ? name : "<unnamed>", expected);
14     return false; // Already initialized or in INITIALIZING/DEAD state
15   }
16 
17   /* Winner: initialize sync primitive if configured */
18   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
19     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initializing mutex", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
20     mutex_init(lc->sync.mutex, name);
21   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
22     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initializing rwlock", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
23     rwlock_init(lc->sync.rwlock, name);
24   } else {
25     log_dev("[lifecycle] init: %s initialized (no sync primitive)", name ? name : "<unnamed>");
26   }
27 
28   return true;
29 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), log_dev,
    mutex_init(), and rwlock_init().

  lifecycle_init_abort (lifecycle_t * lc)

bool lifecycle_init_once (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 31 of file lifecycle.c.


31                                           {
32   if (lc == NULL) {
33     log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: NULL lifecycle pointer");
34     return false;
35   }
36 
37   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED;
38   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING)) {
39     // If already initialized, just return false (no work needed)
40     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED) {
41       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: already initialized");
42       return false;
43     }
44 
45     // If dead, never allow re-init
46     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
47       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: module is dead, no re-init allowed");
48       return false;
49     }
50 
51     // If initializing, don't spin (caller may be retrying or it may complete asynchronously)
52     // Just return false to indicate this thread doesn't need to do init work
53     if (expected == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
54       log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: already initializing, skipping (will resolve asynchronously)");
55       return false;
56     }
57 
58     // Unexpected state
59     log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: unexpected state: %d", expected);
60     return false;
61   }
62 
63   // Winner: state is now LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING
64   // Caller must call lifecycle_init_commit() or lifecycle_init_abort()
65   log_dev("[lifecycle] init_once: won CAS, transitioned to INITIALIZING");
66   return true;
67 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), and log_dev.


Referenced by asciichat_instr_coverage_enabled(),
    asciichat_instr_runtime_get(), audio_client_init(),
    log_search_init(), and splash_set_update_notification().

142     return false;
143   return atomic_load_u64(&lc->state) == LIFECYCLE_DEAD;
144 }

bool lifecycle_reset (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 146 of file lifecycle.c.


146                                       {
147   if (lc == NULL) {
148     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: NULL lifecycle pointer");
149     return false;
150   }
151 
152   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
153   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED)) {
154     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: not in INITIALIZED state (current: %d)", expected);
155     return false; // Not in INITIALIZED state
156   }
157 
158   /* Winner: destroy sync primitive if configured (reset keeps same primitive) */
159   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
160     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: destroying and resetting mutex");
161     mutex_destroy(lc->sync.mutex);
162   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
163     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: destroying and resetting rwlock");
164     rwlock_destroy(lc->sync.rwlock);
165   } else {
166     log_dev("[lifecycle] reset: completed (no sync primitive, allows reinit)");
167   }
168 
169   return true;
170 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), log_dev, and
    mutex_destroy().


Referenced by client_crypto_init().






bool lifecycle_shutdown (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 87 of file lifecycle.c.


87                                          {
88   if (lc == NULL) {
89     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: NULL lifecycle pointer");
90     return false;
91   }
92   uint64_t expected = LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
93   if (!atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &expected, LIFECYCLE_UNINITIALIZED)) {
94     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: not in INITIALIZED state (current: %d)", expected);
95     return false; // Not initialized or in unexpected state
96   }
97 
98   /* Winner: destroy sync primitive if configured */
99   if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX && lc->sync.mutex != NULL) {
100     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: destroying mutex");
101     mutex_destroy(lc->sync.mutex);
102   } else if (lc->sync_type == LIFECYCLE_SYNC_RWLOCK && lc->sync.rwlock != NULL) {
103     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: destroying rwlock");
104     rwlock_destroy(lc->sync.rwlock);
105   } else {
106     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown: completed (no sync primitive)");
107   }
108 
109   return true;
110 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), log_dev, and
    mutex_destroy().

    crypto_client_cleanup(), log_destroy(),
    log_search_destroy(), named_destroy(), named_init(),

    platform_cleanup_binary_path_cache(),

bool lifecycle_shutdown_forever (lifecycle_t * lc)


Definition at line 112 of file lifecycle.c.


112                                                  {
113   if (lc == NULL) {
114     log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: NULL lifecycle pointer");
115     return false;
116   }
117 
118   uint64_t current;
119   do {
120     current = atomic_load_u64(&lc->state);
121     if (current == LIFECYCLE_DEAD) {
122       log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: already DEAD");
123       return false;
124     }
125     if (current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING) {
126       log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: spinning on INITIALIZING");
127     }
128   } while (current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZING || !atomic_cas_u64(&lc->state, &current, LIFECYCLE_DEAD));
129 
130   log_dev("[lifecycle] shutdown_forever: transitioned to DEAD (was in state: %d)", current);
131   return current == LIFECYCLE_INITIALIZED;
132 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), atomic_load_u64(), and
    log_dev.
